My core expertise lies in platforms like AWS, UNIX/Linux, IBM Tivoli, Red Hat, Oracle, MongoDB, and big data distributed computing. I'm passionate about leveraging the power of machine learning and open-source technologies to drive innovation.  My current focus is on utilizing Kubernetes to optimize the execution of AI/ML workloads. I'm an active member of the tech community, co-organizing the DC Kubernetes Meetup and sharing my insights on open-source technologies.

person.name: Swan Pyae Tun
person.bio: Swan Pyae Tun is a DevOps enthusiast, who continuously learn about emerging technologies. He can architect, build, and maintain secure, reliable, and cost optimized systems.

person.name: Tarlan Huseynov
person.bio: Principal DevOps Engineer with a rich background in designing and implementing scalable, cloud-native infrastructure solutions. Known for a strategic approach in optimizing workflows and enhancing system reliability. Passionate about leveraging cloud technologies with IaC and coding expertise to drive innovation and efficiency.

person.name: Paramaguru Subramaniyam
person.bio: I am specialized IT professional who bridges the gap between software development and IT operations. Learn on new tool in DevOps World.

person.name: Sanskar Gurdasani
person.bio: Software Engineer at Accuknox and a recent graduate in Information Technology Engineering from Jabalpur Engineering College. He previously worked as a DevOps Engineer Intern at Scoutflo, where he developed MCP tools for various CNCF projects, including Kubernetes, Prometheus, Grafana, ArgoCD, and Istio. He was an LFX Spring Mentee 2024 for the CNCF – Kyverno, where he developed the Kyverno-Envoy plugin. He had worked as a DevOps Engineer Intern at Cloudraft. Sanskar is a LiFT Scholar, a Kyverno Community Member, and an open-source contributor to various projects, including Kyverno, Kubescape, Nix, Envoy, and more.

person.name: Win Kay Kay Khine
person.bio: I lead the end-to-end SDLC, designing and delivering Kubernetes-based, cloud-native platforms using CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, and Argo CD. I work closely with architects to build production-ready, containerized and multi-tenant systems, integrating IoT monitoring for real-time edge visibility. I continuously improve reliability and scalability through testing, code reviews, and cloud-native best practices.

person.name: Guy Menahem
person.bio: Guy was fortunate enough to experience hands-on the entire evolution of cloud-native computing, from bare-metal servers to VMs and ultimately to Kubernetes. He is passionate about sharing his vast knowledge by writing blogs, speaking at global events, creating video content, and contributing to open-source projects by the CNCF. Guy has been dubbed "The Good Guy" by his peers and members of the community for his persistent desire to help others. It’s no wonder then that his day job at Komodor revolves around helping organizations to adopt K8s and solve the challenges that come with it, while his free time is dedicated to building a local community for Solution Architects and maintaining his educational Youtube channel: https://www.youtube.com/@the_good_guyWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador because it allows me to further my mission of sharing knowledge and driving the adoption of cloud-native technologies. As an ambassador, I will have the opportunity to create more resources, collaboration opportunities, and initiatives with the community & end-users to promote adoption and innovation.

person.name: Juraci Paixao Kröhling
person.bio: I'm a software engineer building telemetry tools. I'm active in the OpenTelemetry community and have been one of the Jaeger project maintainers in the past, having also contributed to OpenTracing. I also love to spread knowledge about the tools I work with, having had the pleasure of speaking at KubeCon (EU and NA), OpenSource Summit (EU and NA), FOSDEM, Devoxx, among others. And more recently, I started a side project called Dose de Telemetria, where I create deeply technical content related to observability, monitoring, and telemetry in my native language, Portuguese.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Recognizing that language might be a barrier for people to understand the CNCF ecosystem and projects, I will focus on sharing knowledge in Portuguese. I'm also looking forward to fostering the ecosystem around our observability tooling, with a particular focus on OpenTelemetry but not limited to it.

person.name: Ryan Lee
person.bio: A DevSecOps Engineer experienced in SRE and Cybersecurity. I have had prior dabblings in ground and marine robotics.

person.name: Toh Hong Lie
person.bio: Passionate about cloud computing, DevOps, container orchestration, automation, and cloud-native technologies—and always looking to expand my knowledge and pick up new skills along the way.

person.name: Bernd Schörgers
person.bio: I am a Platform/DevOps engineer with a focus on (on-premise) Kubernetes, GitOps and CI/CD. I am always learning about new tools and concepts in these areas, and love applying them both at home and the workplace!  Additionally I'm one of the operators of the "home-operations" Discord community where like-minded people hang out and discuss. Feel free to join us :)

person.name: Dennis John
person.bio: Dennis is a Technical Architect (Cloud / DevOps) at Infosys.  In his role, he is responsible for designing and developing solutions for clients, mainly cloud and DevOps related.  He specialises in cloud infrastructure, container orchestration, CI/CD pipelines and application modernisation.  He is active in a number of tech meetups in his city, and actively promotes cloud-native and DevOps solutions. Open-source software is another thing he is very passionate about and he believes that the best comes from collaborating with others and giving back to the community, a way to create and learn together.

person.name: Oliver Bähler
person.bio: Solutions Engineer. Kubernetes Runtime/Multi-Tenancy Enthusiast. Project Capsule Maintainer.

person.name: Daekwang Ryu
person.bio: I am Daekwang Ryu from Korea do for a MSA work.  I am interested in working with people from all over the world.

person.name: Jay Koby
person.bio: Jay Koby brings more than a decade of experience working with a variety of organizations and technologies.  He currently works as a Senior Observability Architect at Grafana Labs.

person.name: Mykhailo Tiapko
person.bio: DevOps / Site Reliability Engineer with demonstrated experience, interested in Cloud Architecture, GitOps, Containerization, Security, FinOps, and Web3 technologies. Dedicated to accelerating development processes through reliable and scalable practices, while consistently focusing on delivering business value

person.name: David Auffray
person.bio: I am passionate about cloud-native technologies and all open-source technologies. I am very curious and I like to share everything I discover. I test some cloud-native technologies to help my city community expand its skills. If I discover and/or do not understand something, I do not hesitate to create issues on open-source technologies to improve the documentation or features. I co-organize a Cloud-Native community with partners to highlight cloud-native and surrounding technologies, such as generative AI.

person.name: Dixán Rivas González
person.bio: Since 2007, I’ve worked across Linux, Active Directory, networking and Kubernetes. Curiosity at my core, I push for systems that are reliable, stable, and built to last.

person.name: Josh Gavant
person.bio: Josh is passionate about enabling project builders and end users to succeed with cloud computing. He's a solution architect for Red Hat and leader of CNCF's TAG App Delivery and WG Platforms. He also leads the CNCF Chicago community group and other Chicago-area groups. Check out Josh's blog at https://blog.joshgav.com/!Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm thrilled to continue enabling beginners and experts alike to make the most of cloud computing! As an ambassador I'll be even more empowered to educate and support Chicago-area developers in our local communities. I'm looking forward to sharing knowledge myself, but most of all I'm looking forward to enabling people in my communities to share their knowledge with each other!

person.name: Roy Liang
person.bio: I am a cloud native developer and advocator with over 6 years of experience in cloud native community. I was a cloud native infrastructure SRE and early Kubernetes contributor. I am currently working as a Senior R&D Engineer in Machine Learning Platform team at Tencent. I have participated in multiple cloud native projects and now I am working on cloud native AI infrastructure. I am also a co-author of "Istio Handbook", traslator of "Distributed Machine Learning Pattern" and speaker of KubeCon.

person.name: Halil İbrahim Mengü
person.bio: I am an IT professional with extensive experience in various cloud-native technologies, particularly Docker and Kubernetes, since 2018, and I have been actively exploring the CNCF ecosystem for many years.

person.name: Jackson Ching
person.bio: I am an IT professional with over 20 years of experience specializing in Linux, digital forensics, and end-to-end solution design for enterprise environments. I have led teams in systems integration and mentored engineers, enhancing operational efficiency through cloud, virtualization, and automation expertise. My strategic approach consistently delivers high-quality, secure infrastructure solutions across diverse industries.

person.name: Rafał Czaja
person.bio: I am an experienced platform engineer, specialising in the design, implementation and management of IT infrastructure. I work with tools and technologies such as Kubernetes, Rancher and Jenkins to ensure scalability, security and performance of systems. My skills in automation, monitoring and resource optimisation contribute to efficient infrastructure management and faster service delivery.

person.name: Ragavendra Vigneshwaran R
person.bio: I am a Site Reliability Engineer at IBM based in Chennai, India, specializing in cloud-native platforms, Kubernetes operations, and observability.My work focuses on building scalable Kubernetes platforms, improving observability pipelines, and automating cloud-native infrastructure. I actively share knowledge through technical notes and community interactions to help others learn Kubernetes with practical, real-world insights.

person.name: Celalettin Altinpinar
person.bio: I have been doing Cloud and Container Orchestrations projects for long time, helping customer to adopt new technologies and transform their legacy applications and environment to cloud-native technologies. I am Red Hat Certified Architect for 6 years and doing events for container technologies, container security and Devops. I am also managing Cloud and DevOps team.

person.name: Hammad Umar
person.bio: I am a Platform Engineer specializing in the intersection of cloud-native orchestration and AI infrastructure. My expertise lies in architecting and managing enterprise-grade environments, ranging from Kubernetes clusters to large-scale AI workloads. I bridge the gap between traditional DevOps and the evolving infrastructure requirements of AI-driven enterprises. I am always looking to improve myself in terms of my skills and the impact of my work through continuous learning and career growth.

person.name: Matheus Ulisses Paiva Ambrozio
person.bio: I am a Software Engineering and Quality Manager with over 13 years of experience in software development and system architecture. My journey with Kubernetes began in 2018, and since then, I have attended events like KubeCon in Detroit, Chicago, and Salt Lake City. I am a Certified Backstage Associate (CBA) and have mentored three cohorts on Kubernetes, leading practical workshops and guiding professionals in effectively adopting this technology. Passionate about open source technologies, I explore and implement CNCF projects such as Kubernetes, Backstage, and others to drive innovation and operational efficiency, delivering scalable and high-impact solutions.

person.name: Aditya Samant
person.bio: Aditya is a highly experienced IT professional, currently working as a Site Reliability Architect. He is a former Director of R&D in Microservices and Cloud Native development. He has vast exposure in several major domains like SDK, Security, Public Clouds, CI/CD, Backing Services and Observability, all on an architecture predominantly based on open-source and Kubernetes, by adopting the DevOps culture. He is a member of the Kubernetes GitHub organization via regular contributions to the Kubernetes documentation.

person.name: Cody Galloway
person.bio: Cody is an Event Manager with a focus on Content at The Linux Foundation, and manages the speakers and content for KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events. Prior to joining The Linux Foundation, Cody brings over six years in the event industry and has assisted planners in over one hundred corporate events.
